rules:
Once during each player's turn, that player may put up to 2 Basic Lightning Energy cards from their discard pile into their hand.

You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can't be played.

Pokémon Tools attached to each Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's) have no effect.

attacks.effect: This Pokémon recovers from all Special Conditions.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

flavorText: If it spots a berry tree, it will immediately go to gather berries without a sideways glance—even if it's in the middle of a battle.

attacks.name: Dizzy Punch
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 90 damage for each heads.

flavorText: There are records of a lost human child being raised by a childless Kangaskhan.

attacks.name: Paw-cket Pilfer
attacks.effect: Choose a random card from your opponent's hand. Your opponent reveals that card and shuffles it into their deck.

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

flavorText: All it does is sleep during the daytime. At night, it patrols its territory with its eyes aglow.

attacks.name: Reckless Abandon
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are tails, this Pokémon also does 90 damage to itself.

flavorText: Its whiskers are essential for maintaining its balance. No matter how friendly you are, it will get angry and bite you if you touch its whiskers.

attacks.name: Strong Bash
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, if this Pokémon is damaged by an attack (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon equal to the damage done to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Its ability to deflect any attack led to it being known as the Fighting Master's Shield. It was feared and respected by all.

attacks.name: Deceit
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Supporter card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Torment
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks. During your opponent's next turn, that Pokémon can't use that attack.

flavorText: Feared and loathed by many, it is believed to bring misfortune to all those who see it at night.

attacks.name: Explode Together Now
attacks.effect: This attack does 40 damage for each Pokémon in play that has "Koffing" or "Weezing" in its name (both yours and your opponent's).

flavorText: Top-grade perfume is made using its internal poison gases by diluting them to the highest level.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.effect: This attack does 40 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It emits psychic energy to observe and study what's around it—and what's around it can include things over six miles away.

attacks.name: Astonish
attacks.effect: Choose a random card from your opponent's hand. Your opponent reveals that card and shuffles it into their deck.

attacks.name: Gadget Show
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage for each Pokémon Tool attached to all of your Pokémon.

flavorText: Its electricity-like body can enter some kinds of machines and take control in order to make mischief.
It stores electricity in its fur. It gives off sparks from all over its body in seasons when the air is dry.

attacks.name: Shell Press
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 10 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Clamperl's pearls are exceedingly precious. They can be more than 10 times as costly as Shellder's pearls.

attacks.name: Swim Together
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 3 Misty's Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It ferries people across the sea on its back. It may sing an enchanting cry if it is in a good mood.
It is constantly wracked by a headache. When the headache turns intense, it begins using mysterious powers.

attacks.name: Inferno Kick Flurry
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon. This attack also does 120 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: When facing a tough foe, it looses flames from its wrists. Its powerful legs let it jump clear over buildings.

attacks.name: Cruel Coal
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned and Confused.

attacks.name: Scorching Fire
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: If you are burned by the flames it shoots from its mouth, the pain will never go away.

attacks.name: Buddy Blast
attacks.effect: This attack does 60 more damage for each Ethan's Adventure card in your discard pile.

flavorText: It has a secret, devastating move. It rubs its blazing fur to cause huge explosions.
The fastest runner becomes the leader, and it decides the herd's pace and direction of travel.

attacks.name: Hydra Breath
attacks.effect: Discard 6 Basic Grass Energy cards from your hand, and Knock Out your opponent's Active Pokémon. If you can't discard 6 cards in this way, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Seven syrpents live inside an apple made of syrup. The syrpent in the center is the commander.

attacks.name: Rocket Rush
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage for each of your Team Rocket's Pokémon in play.

flavorText: It clings to branches and ceilings using its threads and moves without a sound. It takes out its prey before the prey even notices it.

attacks.name: Superb Scissors
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Its thick claws are its greatest weapons. They're mighty enough to crack Rhyperior's carapace.
The blooming of Gracidea flowers confers the power of flight upon it. Feelings of gratitude are the message it delivers.
The poison in its right hand is quick acting. The poison in its left hand is slow acting. Both are life threatening.

attacks.name: Whirlwind
attacks.effect: Switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)

flavorText: As Yanma surveys its territory, it periodically stops to hover in place by flapping its wings at high speeds.

rules: This card can only be attached to a Team Rocket's Pokémon. If this card is attached to anything other than a Team Rocket's Pokémon, discard this card.  As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides 2 in any combination of Psychic Energy and Darkness Energy.

Search your deck for up to 3 Pokémon Tool cards that have "Technical Machine" in their name, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Colorless Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent's) have no Abilities.

You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can't be played.

fullDisplayName: Team Rocket's Venture Bomb 179/182 Destined Rivals
rules:
Flip a coin. If heads, put 2 damage counters on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. If tails, put 2 damage counters on your Active Pokémon.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Search your deck for a Supporter card that has "Team Rocket" in its name, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

If you go first, you may use this card during your first turn.  Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Team Rocket's Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Search your deck for a Trainer card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

fullDisplayName: Team Rocket's Great Ball 175/182 Destined Rivals
rules:
Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for an Evolution Team Rocket's Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. If tails, search your deck for a Basic Team Rocket's Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Switch your Active Team Rocket's Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Team Rocket's Pokémon. If you do, switch in 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Once during each player's turn, if they played a Supporter card that has "Team Rocket" in its name from their hand this turn, they may draw 2 cards.

You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can't be played.

Turn 1 of your opponent's face-down Prize cards face up and choose a random card from your opponent's hand. Your opponent reveals that card. You may have your opponent switch those cards. (That Prize card remains face up for the rest of the game.)

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Draw cards until you have 5 cards in your hand. If all of your Pokémon in play are Team Rocket's Pokémon, draw cards until you have 8 cards in your hand instead.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

You can use this card only if any of your Team Rocket's Pokémon were Knocked Out during your opponent's last turn.  Each player shuffles their hand into their deck. Then, you draw 5 cards, and your opponent draws 3 cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Once during each player's turn, that player may search their deck for a Marnie's Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into their hand. Then, that player shuffles their deck.

You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can't be played.

Shuffle up to 5 Pokémon from your discard pile into your deck.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Each player shuffles their hand into their deck and draws 4 cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Steven's Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's) take 30 less damage from attacks from the opponent's Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can't be played.

Search your deck for up to 3 in any combination of Ethan's Pokémon and Basic Fire Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Shuffle up to 5 Basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your deck.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Draw 2 cards. If your opponent has 3 or fewer Prize cards remaining, draw 2 more cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

The Cynthia's Pokémon this card is attached to gets +70 HP.

You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

Heal 30 damage from your Active Pokémon. If that Pokémon is an Arven's Pokémon, heal 100 damage from it instead.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

attacks.name: Push Down
attacks.effect: You may switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)

flavorText: These Pokémon prefer to live in cities. They form flocks based on the color of their feathers, and they fight over territory.
If it spots a berry tree, it will immediately go to gather berries without a sideways glance—even if it's in the middle of a battle.

attacks.name: Gnaw Through
attacks.effect: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tools from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It stores berries in its cheeks. When there are no berries to be found, Skwovet will stuff pebbles into its cheeks to stave off its cravings.

attacks.name: Add On
attacks.effect: Draw 3 cards.

flavorText: It dives at a steep angle as soon as it spots its prey. It catches its prey with sharp claws.
It dislikes cold seasons. They migrate to other lands in search of warmth, flying over 180 miles a day.

attacks.name: R Command
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage for each Supporter card that has "Team Rocket" in its name in your discard pile.

flavorText: An additional program was installed to create a more advanced Pokémon, but it was apparently flawed. Porygon-Z moves oddly as a result.

attacks.name: R Command
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage for each Supporter card that has "Team Rocket" in its name in your discard pile.

flavorText: This artificial Pokémon evolved thanks to cutting- edge science. It sometimes displays behavior that is not in its programming.

attacks.name: Hacking
attacks.effect: Discard a card from your hand. If you do, your opponent discards a card from their hand.

flavorText: It is an artificial Pokémon. Since it doesn't breathe, people are excited by its potential to be useful in any environment.

attacks.name: Clean Hit
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is an Evolution Pokémon, this attack does 50 more damage.

flavorText: When it targets an enemy, it charges furiously while whipping its body with its long tails.

attacks.name: Dizzy Punch
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 90 damage for each heads.

flavorText: There are records of a lost human child being raised by a childless Kangaskhan.

attacks.name: Metal Slash
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: It is formed by two Beldum joining together. Its two brains are linked, amplifying its psychic power.
The magnetic force generated by its body repels the ground's natural magnetism, letting it float.

attacks.name: Sonic Double
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 damage to 2 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Its sturdy, heavy-looking iron body is actually thin and light, so it can fly at speeds over 180 mph.

attacks.name: Roost
attacks.effect: Heal 50 damage from this Pokémon. During your next turn, this Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: Its sturdy, heavy-looking iron body is actually thin and light, so it can fly at speeds over 180 mph.

attacks.name: Iron Shake-Up
attacks.effect: You may move any amount of Metal Energy from your Pokémon to your other Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Hurricane of Needles
attacks.effect: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 80 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It's usually found hanging on to a fat tree trunk. It shoots out bits of its shell when it sees action.

attacks.name: Vigorous Tackle
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has no damage counters on it, this attack does 120 more damage.

attacks.name: Boss Headbutt
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Boss Headbutt.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

flavorText: It always scowls in an attempt to make opponents take it seriously, but even crying children will burst into laughter when they see Maschiff's face.

attacks.name: Spiky Wheel
attacks.effect: This attack does 40 more damage for each Darkness Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Morpeko eats seeds constantly to keep its hunger at bay. If it's hungry for too long, it grows wild and brutish.

attacks.name: Shadow Bullet
attacks.effect: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

flavorText: Morgrem prefers dirty tactics, like ambushing, because it doesn't have confidence in its brawn.

attacks.name: Filch
attacks.effect: Draw a card.

flavorText: The reason this Pokémon causes trouble for those it feels close to is because Impidimp itself gets irritable if it can't absorb negative emotions.

attacks.name: Wild Tackle
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: Groups of them beat up anything that enters their territory. Each can spit acidic liquid from its mouth.

attacks.name: Crunch
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It stretches its saggy skin up to its neck to protect itself. Among Scraggy, individuals with saggier skin apparently garner more respect.

attacks.name: Pointy Claws
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex, this attack does 70 more damage.

flavorText: This stealthy Pokémon sneaks up behind prey without making any sound at all. It competes with Thievul for territory.

attacks.name: Pointy Nails
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex, this attack does 40 more damage.

flavorText: Opponents that get drawn in by its adorable behavior come away with stinging scratches from its claws and stinging pride from its laughter.

attacks.name: Raging Curse
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 damage for each damage counter on all of your Benched Cynthia's Pokémon. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness.

flavorText: Its constant mischief and misdeeds resulted in it being bound to an Odd Keystone by a mysterious spell.

attacks.name: Strike the Sleeper
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon for each damage counter on that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: This cunning Pokémon hides under the cover of darkness, waiting to attack its prey.

attacks.name: Torment
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks. During your opponent's next turn, that Pokémon can't use that attack.

attacks.name: Explode Together Now
attacks.effect: This attack does 40 damage for each Pokémon in play that has "Koffing" or "Weezing" in its name (both yours and your opponent's).

flavorText: Top-grade perfume is made using its internal poison gases by diluting them to the highest level.
Toxic gas is held within its thin, balloon-shaped body, so it can cause massive explosions.

attacks.name: Gooped Up
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused. During your opponent's next turn, that Pokémon can't retreat.

attacks.name: Hazardous Venom
attacks.effect: This attack does 100 damage for each Special Condition affecting your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It's thickly covered with a filthy, vile sludge. It is so toxic, even its footprints contain poison.

attacks.name: Corrosive Sludge
attacks.effect: At the end of your opponent's next turn, discard the Defending Pokémon and all attached cards.

flavorText: Born from sludge, these Pokémon now gather in polluted places and increase the bacteria in their bodies.

fullDisplayName: Team Rocket's Crobat ex 122/182 Destined Rivals

attacks.name: Assassin's Return
attacks.effect: You may put this Pokémon into your hand. (Discard all cards attached to this Pokémon.)

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Its feet are tiny, but this Pokémon walks skillfully. It sneaks up on sleeping prey before sinking in its fangs and slurping up blood.

attacks.name: Poison Spray
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Zubat live in caves, down where the sun's light won't reach. In the morning, they gather together to keep each other warm as they sleep.

fullDisplayName: Team Rocket's Nidoking ex 119/182 Destined Rivals

attacks.name: Tainted Horn
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. During Pokémon Checkup, put 8 damage counters on that Pokémon instead of 1.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Horn Rend
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: It's nervous and quick to act aggressively. The potency of its poison increases along with the level of adrenaline present in its body.
Small but brave, this Pokémon will hold its ground and even risk its life in battle to protect the female it's friendly with.

attacks.name: Love Impact
attacks.effect: If a Pokémon that has "Nidoking" in its name is on your Bench, this attack does 120 more damage.

flavorText: It pacifies offspring by placing them in the gaps between the spines on its back. The spines will never secrete poison while young are present.

attacks.name: Dark Awakening
attacks.effect: Choose up to 2 of your Darkness Pokémon. For each of those Pokémon, search your deck for a card that evolves from that Pokémon and put it onto that Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: If the group is threatened, these Pokémon will band together to assault enemies with a chorus of ultrasonic waves.

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It uses its hard incisor teeth to crush and eat berries. The tip of a female Nidoran's horn is a bit more rounded than the tip of a male's horn.

attacks.name: Spinning Tail
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: The pattern on its belly appears to be a frightening face. Weak foes will flee just at the sight of the pattern.

attacks.name: Drag Down
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It can freely detach its jaw to swallow large prey whole. It can become too heavy to move, however.

attacks.name: Rock Kagura
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Basic Fighting Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Mountain Ramming
attacks.effect: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

flavorText: This form has excellent defenses, absorbing impacts solidly like the cornerstones that support houses.

attacks.name: Pull
attacks.effect: Switch in 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot.

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: These Pokémon gather into groups and form colonies deep within forests. They absolutely hate it when strangers approach.

attacks.name: Slight Intrusion
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: Toedscool lives in muggy forests. The flaps that fall from its body are chewy and very delicious.
This Pokémon has been treasured not just for its physical labor but also because it produces high-quality mud used for making pottery.

attacks.name: Running Charge
attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 40 damage for each heads.

flavorText: This Pokémon covers itself in mud that it has regurgitated. The mud won't dry out even if it's exposed to the sun for a long time.

attacks.name: Super Sandstorm
attacks.effect: This attack also does 40 damage to each Benched Pokémon that has any damage counters on it (both yours and your opponent's). (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It brandishes its gaping mouth in a display of fearsome strength. It raises vast quantities of sand while attacking.

attacks.name: Push Down
attacks.effect: Switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)

flavorText: It shrouds itself in sand to ward off germs. It travels easily through the sands of the desert.

attacks.name: Corkscrew Dive
attacks.effect: You may draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.

attacks.name: Draconic Buster
attacks.effect: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

flavorText: In rare cases, it molts and sheds its scales. Medicine containing its scales as an ingredient will make a weary body feel invigorated.

attacks.name: Rock Hurl
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: It skulks in caves, and when prey or an enemy passes by, it leaps out and chomps them. The force of its attack sometimes chips its teeth.

attacks.name: Regi Charge
attacks.effect: Attach up to 2 Basic Fighting Energy cards from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Giant Rock
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Stage 2 Pokémon, this attack does 140 more damage.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Harmonious Spirit Palm
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon and your opponent's Active Pokémon have the same amount of Energy attached, this attack does 120 more damage.

flavorText: Through yoga training, it gained the psychic power to predict its foe's next move.

attacks.name: Steady Punch
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: It never skips its daily yoga training. It heightens its inner strength through meditation.

attacks.name: Mountain Drop
attacks.effect: If a Stadium is in play, this attack does 70 more damage.

flavorText: It controls three units called Mini-Noses using magnetic force. With them, it can attack a foe from three directions.
The magnet in Nosepass's nose provides an unerring compass, making this Pokémon an excellent partner for Trainers going on a journey.

attacks.name: Demolition Tackle
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Extremely strong, it can change the landscape. It is so insolent that it doesn't care about others.

attacks.name: Explosive Ascension
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: This pupa flies around wildly by venting with great force the gas pressurized inside its body.

attacks.name: Mountain Munch
attacks.effect: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

flavorText: Born deep underground, this Pokémon becomes a pupa after eating enough dirt to make a mountain.

attacks.name: Impound
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

attacks.name: Try to Imitate
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks and use it as this attack.

flavorText: Although it always pretends to be a tree, its composition appears more similar to rock than to vegetation.

attacks.name: Impact Blow
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Impact Blow.

flavorText: When its anger rose beyond a critical point, this Pokémon gained power that is unfettered by the limits of its physical body.

attacks.name: Drag Off
attacks.effect: Switch in 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot. This attack does 30 damage to the new Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It becomes wildly furious if it even senses someone looking at it. It chases anyone that meets its glare.

attacks.name: Wild Kick
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It lives in groups in the treetops. If it loses sight of its group, it becomes infuriated by its loneliness.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.effect: This attack does 40 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It emits psychic energy to observe and study what's around it—and what's around it can include things over six miles away.

attacks.name: Disruptive Radar
attacks.effect: Look at the top 5 cards of your opponent's deck and put them back in any order.

flavorText: As it grows inside its shell, it uses its psychic abilities to monitor the outside world and prepare for evolution.

attacks.name: Gemstone Mimicry
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of your opponent's Active Tera Pokémon's attacks and use it as this attack.

flavorText: This Pokémon lives in dark places untouched by sunlight. When it appears before humans, it hides itself under a cloth that resembles a Pikachu.
It has slept underground for hundreds of millions of years since its birth. It's occasionally found during the excavation of caves.

attacks.name: Chiming Commotion
attacks.effect: Discard a random card from your opponent's hand.

flavorText: There is an orb inside its mouth. When it hops, the orb bounces all over and makes a ringing sound.

attacks.name: Eerie Light
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Clay Blast
attacks.effect: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It appears to have been born from clay dolls made by ancient people. It uses telekinesis to float and move.

attacks.name: Summoning Sign
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Steven's Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It was discovered in ancient ruins. While moving, it constantly spins. It stands on one foot even when asleep.

attacks.name: Rocket Mirror
attacks.effect: Move all damage counters from 1 of your Benched Team Rocket's Pokémon to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: To keep its pitch-black tail hidden, it lives quietly in the darkness. It is never first to attack.

fullDisplayName: Team Rocket's Mewtwo ex 081/182 Destined Rivals

attacks.name: Erasure Ball
attacks.effect: You may discard up to 2 Energy from your Benched Pokémon. This attack does 60 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Bench Manipulation
attacks.effect: Your opponent flips a coin for each of their Benched Pokémon. This attack does 80 damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon for each tails. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

flavorText: When it locks eyes with an enemy, it will use a mix of psi moves, such as Hypnosis and Confusion.

attacks.name: Hypnotic Ray
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It remembers every dream it eats. It rarely eats the dreams of adults because children's are much tastier.

attacks.name: Thunder Raid
attacks.effect: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon, and this attack does 210 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon ex. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Electricity sparks from the pads on its limbs. Wherever Zeraora runs, lightning flashes and thunder echoes.

attacks.name: Astonish
attacks.effect: Choose a random card from your opponent's hand. Your opponent reveals that card and shuffles it into their deck.

attacks.name: Gadget Show
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage for each Pokémon Tool attached to all of your Pokémon.

flavorText: Its electricity-like body can enter some kinds of machines and take control in order to make mischief.

attacks.name: Flash Impact
attacks.effect: This attack also does 40 damage to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It rarely appears before people. It is said to nest where lightning has fallen.
It stores electricity in its fur. It gives off sparks from all over its body in seasons when the air is dry.
The bright light on its tail can be seen far away. It has been treasured since ancient times as a beacon.

attacks.name: Thunder Shock
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: As a result of storing too much electricity, it developed patches where even downy wool won't grow.

attacks.name: Procurement
attacks.effect: Search your deck for an Item card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: If static electricity builds in its body, its fleece doubles in volume. Touching it will shock you.

attacks.name: Zapping Draw
attacks.effect: Draw a card.

flavorText: It is unskilled at storing electric power. Any kind of shock causes it to discharge energy spontaneously.

attacks.name: Jamming Wing
attacks.effect: You may move an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon to 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Wicked Thunder
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has any Team Rocket's Energy attached, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: Zapdos is a legendary bird Pokémon. It's said that when Zapdos rubs its feathers together, lightning will fall immediately after.

attacks.name: Dual Bolt
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 damage to 2 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: High-Voltage Press
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has at least 2 extra Energy attached (in addition to this attack's cost), this attack does 100 more damage.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

flavorText: Its body constantly discharges electricity. Getting close to it will make your hair stand on end.

attacks.name: Water Kagura
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Basic Water Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Bubble Drain
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: This form excels in both attack and defense. It ceaselessly unleashes moves like a spring gushes water.

attacks.name: Avenging Billow
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Dynamic Dive
attacks.effect: You may do 120 more damage. If you do, this Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Crushing Press
attacks.effect: You may discard a Stadium in play. If you do, this attack does 140 more damage.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

flavorText: This species left the ocean and began living on land a very long time ago. It seems to be closely related to Wailmer.

attacks.name: Dive
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It spins its tail fins to leap from the water, then viciously bites down on Wingull flying close to the water's surface.

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: Arrokuda can swim quickly only in a straight line. The ones that become sluggish from overeating are the first to be targeted by flocks of Wattrel.

attacks.name: Manual Wash
attacks.effect: Heal 10 damage from each of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Gadget Show
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage for each Pokémon Tool attached to all of your Pokémon.

flavorText: This Rotom has entered a washing machine. It nods with satisfaction after it floods the surrounding area.

attacks.name: Frozen Wood
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has 2 or more Grass Energy attached, this attack does 120 more damage.

flavorText: It lives a quiet life on mountains that are perpetually covered in snow. It hides itself by whipping up blizzards.
During cold seasons, it migrates to the mountain's lower reaches. It returns to the snow-covered summit in the spring.

attacks.name: Whirlpool
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Aqua Slash
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: With its flotation sac inflated, it can carry people on its back. It deflates the sac before it dives.
It spins its two tails like a screw to propel itself through water. The tails also slice clinging seaweed.

attacks.name: Crescendo Wave
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage for each Water Energy attached to this Pokémon. Before doing damage, you may attach any number of Basic Water Energy cards from your hand to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It sucks bodily fluids out of its prey. The leftover meat sinks to the seafloor, where it becomes food for other Pokémon.
Deep seas are their habitat. According to tradition, when Huntail wash up onshore, something unfortunate will happen.

attacks.name: Shell Press
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 10 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Clamperl's pearls are exceedingly precious. They can be more than 10 times as costly as Shellder's pearls.

attacks.name: Aqua Split
attacks.effect: This attack also does 30 damage to 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Its lovely scales are described as rainbow colored. They change color depending on the viewing angle.

attacks.name: Undulate
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

flavorText: This tough Pokémon is perfectly fine even in dirty water. However, due to its ragged, shabby appearance, it isn't popular.

attacks.name: Dark Frost
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has any Team Rocket's Energy attached, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: This Pokémon can control ice at will. Articuno is said to live in snowy mountains riddled with permafrost.

attacks.name: Swim Together
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 3 Misty's Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It ferries people across the sea on its back. It may sing an enchanting cry if it is in a good mood.

attacks.name: Splashing Panic
attacks.effect: Discard the top 7 cards of your deck, and this attack does 70 damage for each Misty's Pokémon that you discarded in this way.

flavorText: Once it appears, it goes on a rampage. It remains enraged until it demolishes everything around it.
An underpowered, pathetic Pokémon. It may jump high on rare occasions but never more than seven feet.

attacks.name: Abrupt Flash
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon evolved from Misty's Staryu during this turn, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: Starmie swims by spinning its body at high speed. As this Pokémon cruises through the ocean, it absorbs tiny plankton.

attacks.name: Bubble Beam
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Fish Pokémon nibble at it, but Staryu isn't bothered. Its body regenerates quickly, even if part of it is completely torn off.
It is constantly wracked by a headache. When the headache turns intense, it begins using mysterious powers.

attacks.name: Fire Kagura
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Basic Fire Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Searing Flame
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: This form is the most aggressive, bombarding enemies with the intensity of flames blazing within a hearth.

attacks.name: Singe
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Gadget Show
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage for each Pokémon Tool attached to all of your Pokémon.

flavorText: If the convection microwave oven is not working properly, then the Rotom inhabiting it will become lethargic.

attacks.name: Inferno Kick Flurry
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon. This attack also does 120 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: When facing a tough foe, it looses flames from its wrists. Its powerful legs let it jump clear over buildings.

attacks.name: Double Kick
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It boosts its concentration by emitting harsh cries. Its kicks have outstanding destructive power.

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.effect: Draw a card.

flavorText: Torchic feels toasty warm if you hug it. It has a flame sac inside its belly, and the flames burn continuously as long as Torchic has life in it.

attacks.name: Shining Feathers
attacks.effect: Heal 50 damage from each of your Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Cruel Coal
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned and Confused.

attacks.name: Scorching Fire
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: If you are burned by the flames it shoots from its mouth, the pain will never go away.
It is smart enough to hunt in packs. It uses a variety of cries for communicating with others.

attacks.name: Lava Burst
attacks.effect: Discard up to 5 Fire Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 70 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

flavorText: This Pokémon lives near the craters of volcanoes. It stores fire energy in its shell of cooled and hardened magma.
Its lava body can cool and chip away at times, but a magma bath will heal it right up.

attacks.name: Buddy Blast
attacks.effect: This attack does 60 more damage for each Ethan's Adventure card in your discard pile.

flavorText: It has a secret, devastating move. It rubs its blazing fur to cause huge explosions.
Before battle, it turns its back on its opponent to demonstrate how ferociously its fire blazes.

attacks.name: Ember
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It usually stays hunched over. If it is angry or surprised, it shoots flames out of its back.

fullDisplayName: Team Rocket's Moltres ex 031/182 Destined Rivals

attacks.name: Flame Screen
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 50 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Evil Incineration
attacks.effect: Discard a Team Rocket's Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, discard your opponent's Active Pokémon and all attached cards.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

flavorText: The fastest runner becomes the leader, and it decides the herd's pace and direction of travel.

attacks.name: Double Headbutt
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: If you've been accepted by Ponyta, its burning mane is mysteriously no longer hot to the touch.

attacks.name: Punishing Fang
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Darkness Pokémon, this attack does 100 more damage.

flavorText: An ancient picture scroll shows that people were captivated by its movement as it ran through prairies.
It has a brave and trustworthy nature. It fearlessly stands up to bigger and stronger foes.

attacks.name: Grass Kagura
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Basic Grass Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Ogre's Hammer
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Ogre's Hammer.

flavorText: This Pokémon's type changes based on which mask it's wearing. It confounds its enemies with nimble movements and kicks.

attacks.name: Upside-Down Draw
attacks.effect: Draw 3 cards from the bottom of your deck.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.effect: This attack does 90 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.effect: Draw a card.

flavorText: It rolls its mud ball around while the energy it needs for evolution matures. Eventually the time comes for it to evolve.

attacks.name: Oil Salvo
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon 6 times. (You can choose the same Pokémon more than once.) For each time you chose a Pokémon, do 20 damage to it. This damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

attacks.name: Aroma Shot
attacks.effect: This Pokémon recovers from all Special Conditions.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Nutrients
attacks.effect: Heal 40 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.

flavorText: Dolliv shares its tasty, fresh-scented oil with others. This species has coexisted with humans since times long gone.
It protects itself from enemies by emitting oil from the fruit on its head. This oil is bitter and astringent enough to make someone flinch.

attacks.name: Rocket Rush
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage for each of your Team Rocket's Pokémon in play.

flavorText: It clings to branches and ceilings using its threads and moves without a sound. It takes out its prey before the prey even notices it.

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: The ball of threads wrapped around its body is elastic enough to deflect the scythes of Scyther, this Pokémon's natural enemy.

attacks.name: Hydra Breath
attacks.effect: Discard 6 Basic Grass Energy cards from your hand, and Knock Out your opponent's Active Pokémon. If you can't discard 6 cards in this way, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Seven syrpents live inside an apple made of syrup. The syrpent in the center is the commander.

attacks.name: Energy Loop
attacks.effect: Put an Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand.

flavorText: Dipplin is two creatures in one Pokémon. Its evolution was triggered by a special apple grown only in one place.

attacks.name: Mini Drain
attacks.effect: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It grows by eating the flesh of an apple. Applin uses its own body fluid to strengthen the apple's skin and prevent it from rotting.

attacks.name: Searching Eyes
attacks.effect: Look at 1 of your opponent's face-down Prize cards.

flavorText: Often found in gardens, this Pokémon has hairs on its body that it uses to assess its surroundings.

attacks.name: Petal Blade Dance
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Basic Grass Energy cards from your hand. If you can't discard 2 cards in this way, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: By masquerading as a bug Pokémon, it lowers the guard of actual bug Pokémon lured in by a scent of sweet flowers. Its sickles bring them down.
Fomantis hates having its naps interrupted. It fires off beams using energy it gathers by bathing in the sun.

attacks.name: Superb Scissors
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Its thick claws are its greatest weapons. They're mighty enough to crack Rhyperior's carapace.

attacks.name: Ascension
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It first tries to find a rock to live in, but if there are no suitable rocks to be found, Dwebble may move in to the ports of a Hippowdon.
The blooming of Gracidea flowers confers the power of flight upon it. Feelings of gratitude are the message it delivers.

attacks.name: Trimming Mower
attacks.effect: Discard a Stadium in play.

attacks.name: Gadget Show
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage for each Pokémon Tool attached to all of your Pokémon.

flavorText: The lawn mower is one of the household appliances that led to the development of the Rotom Dex.
The poison in its right hand is quick acting. The poison in its left hand is slow acting. Both are life threatening.
It uses the different poisons in each hand separately when it attacks. The stronger its aroma, the healthier it is.

attacks.name: Damage Rush
attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 50 more damage for each heads.

attacks.name: Mega Drain
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It scatters poisonous spores and throws powerful punches while its foe is hampered by inhaled spores.
It prefers damp places. By day it remains still in the forest shade. It releases toxic powder from its head.
It likes to make its shell thicker by adding layers of tree bark. The additional weight doesn't bother it.

attacks.name: Jet Cyclone
attacks.effect: Move 3 Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Whirlwind
attacks.effect: Switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)

flavorText: As Yanma surveys its territory, it periodically stops to hover in place by flapping its wings at high speeds.

attacks.name: Rallying Horn
attacks.effect: If any of your Ethan's Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an attack during your opponent's last turn, this attack does 100 more damage.

flavorText: This Pokémon clamps its pincers down on its prey and then either splits the prey in half or flings it away.
